note.text: Patient reports severe reaction with urticaria and respiratory distress when given penicillin as a child. Avoid all penicillin-based antibiotics.
Patient developed maculopapular rash after sulfa antibiotic for UTI. Documented to guide antibiotic selection.
Patient reports occasional mild GI upset and urticaria after eating shrimp. Not anaphylactic. Patient chooses to avoid.

description.text: Prevent progression from prediabetes to Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus

note.text: Patient demonstrates good adherence to glucose monitoring. HbA1c trending stable in prediabetic range. Continue current management plan with lifestyle modifications and quarterly monitoring.

description.text: Reduce chronic low back pain to manageable levels using non-opioid approaches

coding.display: Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported

note.text: Pain scores fluctuate between 3-6/10. Patient engaged in PT. Continue conservative management with NSAIDs, physical therapy, and core strengthening.
Serial CBCs show mild persistent anemia. Continue iron supplementation and dietary counseling. Further workup planned if no improvement.

description.text: Prevent unintended pregnancy while managing chronic health conditions

note.text: Patient adherent to oral contraceptive pill. Expressed interest in planning future pregnancy once prediabetes and pain are optimally managed.

dosageInstruction.text: Take one tablet orally once daily at same time each day

note.text: Patient picking up routine refill of contraceptive. Counseled on importance of daily adherence and backup methods.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet by mouth every 6-8 hours as needed for pain. Do not exceed 3 tablets per day.

note.text: Prescribed for chronic low back pain management. Patient counseled on GI side effects and taking with food.
Annual metabolic screening for patient with prediabetes. Monitor glucose, renal function, electrolytes.

coding.display: Complete blood count (hemogram) panel - Blood by Automated count

note.text: Evaluate persistent fatigue and previously documented anemia.
Quarterly monitoring per diabetes prevention protocol.

reasonCode.text: First prenatal visit, routine prenatal screening

note.text: Initial prenatal labs including blood type, Rh, antibody screen, infectious disease screening.
Sample quality adequate. No hemolysis noted. Used for Comprehensive Metabolic Panel.
Sample run on automated hematology analyzer within 4 hours of collection. Used for Complete Blood Count.
Adequate cellularity for interpretation. Liquid-based cytology preparation.
First morning specimen. Positive pregnancy test result.

note.text: Patient wishes to delay pregnancy until chronic health conditions are optimally managed. Discussed preconception planning.

note.text: Patient reports improved health status, interested in preconception counseling for future planned pregnancy.
Patient works as registered nurse in medical-surgical unit. Full-time employment. Relevant for occupational health exposures and physical demands contributing to back pain.
Afebrile at routine wellness visit.

note.text: Good oxygenation on supplemental oxygen via nasal cannula at 2 L/min.

coding.display: Goals, preferences, and priorities for medical treatment [Reported]

note.text: Patient expresses preference for lifestyle modifications, physical therapy, and conservative management before escalating to medications. Requests shared decision-making for treatment plans.

coding.display: Goals, preferences, and priorities for medical treatment [Reported]

resource.valueString: Patient prefers non-pharmacological interventions when possible. Requests lifestyle modifications, physical therapy, and conservative management before escalating to medications. Values shared decision-making for all treatment plans.

Prefers female providers when possible due to history of intimate partner violence. Values continuity of care and long-term relationships with care team. Prefers clear explanations and involvement in decision-making.

note.text: Given history of intimate partner violence, patient requests female providers for sensitive examinations. Values long-term relationships with care team. Prefers clear explanations and involvement in decision-making.

resource.conclusion: No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality. Heart size normal. Lungs clear.
Normal sinus rhythm. No acute ischemic changes. Normal QRS duration.
